text.title: IaC DRY Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules plus a skip-clause for `IaC DRY Patterns`. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Factor a pattern only after the third concrete instance; premature abstraction is forbidden.

rationale.source: Refactoring: Improving the Design of Existing Code (Fowler)

rule.rationale: Two instances rarely carry enough variation signal; abstracting too early bakes in wrong axis of variation.

rule.statement: Repeated resource blocks with shared shape MUST use for_each / dynamic blocks; copy-paste duplication is forbidden once rule-of-three triggers.
rule.rationale: Copy-paste blocks drift; for_each enforces single-shape contract.

rule.statement: Shared constants (tags, names, prefixes) MUST live in locals block at the root, not repeated as literals inside each resource.
rule.rationale: Literals drift; locals are the single source.

rule.statement: Per-environment differences MUST be expressed as *.tfvars / Pulumi stack-config overlays on a shared module, not by copying the module per env.
rule.rationale: Per-env copies double the change surface and decay independently.

rule.statement: Numeric constants with semantic meaning (timeouts, replicas, ports) MUST be named via variable or locals; raw numbers in resource blocks are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Bare numbers obscure intent + cause silent inconsistencies.

rule.statement: If Applies If preconditions fail OR any Skip If trigger fires, do not run this methodology.
